title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect involved in an armed robbery on Jan. 22 in Dorchester.
The robbery occurred around 4:30 p.m. near Adams and Whitten Streets, targeting multiple victims at gunpoint.
Police urge anyone with information to contact 617-343-4335 or the anonymous CrimeStoppers tip line.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials may withhold the entertainment license unless $7.8 million in public safety funds are provided up front.
The Boston FIFA World Cup 2026 host committee promises to cover costs post-game, with support for shortfalls from Kraft Sports and Entertainment.
Massachusetts is awaiting over $46 million in federal security funding as the World Cup approaches, causing concerns in host cities.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will hold her first 'office hours' meeting for Boston residents on Thursday in Roxbury.
The event aims to address city issues directly with residents and will include staff from over a dozen city departments.
City services available include voting registration and obtaining birth, marriage, and death certificates.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins consider acquiring players like Robert Thomas and Simon Nemec before the 2026 NHL trade deadline.
Robert Thomas of the St. Louis Blues is a key target, but acquiring him could require significant assets from the Bruins.
Simon Nemec is another potential addition, offering future potential for the Bruins' defense as a 22-year-old blueliner.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, of Pawtucket, R.I., was indicted for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm at a Franklin residential program.
Allison, a staffer at Amego, Inc., allegedly used improper restraint techniques, resulting in serious injury to the student.
The incident was captured on security footage, and Allison has been suspended pending further investigation and court proceedings.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, from Charlotte, NC, is running the Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Lewis was inspired to run after losing her friend Lily to brain cancer and seeing her father participate in the marathon.
Her goal is to raise funds for cancer research, dedicating every run to those affected by cancer.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes, 55, pleaded guilty to rape and child pornography charges in Fall River Superior Court.
Dos Santos Mendes was sentenced to eight to ten years in state prison and will be deported to Brazil post-sentence.
Police found child pornography on Mendes' phones, supporting prosecution claims of his criminal activities.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots releasing Stefon Diggs to free up $17 million in cap space ahead of the 2026 NFL season.
The team is considering signing free agent Alec Pierce or trading for stars like A.J. Brown as options.
Possible draft picks include Texas A&M's KC Concepcion and Washington's Denzel Boston to fill the vacancy.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler from Granby is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in support of Boston Children's Hospital's Miles for Miracles.
Her daughter received a life-saving liver transplant at Boston Children's Hospital, inspiring Poehler's participation in the marathon.
The run aims to raise funds for research, treatments, and compassionate care for children facing medical challenges.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 54% of 350 readers support U.S. strikes on Iran, 43% oppose, and 3% are unsure.
Supporters cite Iran's regional conflict role and regime's repression as reasons for their stance.
Opponents fear escalation into broader war and criticize lack of congressional approval for strikes.
